key_insight: A fully variational CutFEM formulation for finite-strain elasticity can be built entirely from an augmented energy functional, making the method model-independent and compatible with automatic differentiation.
engineering_relevance: This gives a robust unfitted-solid mechanics workflow that weakly enforces boundary conditions, handles complex geometries without remeshing, and preserves optimal convergence and conditioning.
method: Augment the hyperelastic bulk energy with Nitsche boundary terms and ghost-penalty stabilization.
Derive residuals and the symmetrized tangent by successive variation of the energy functional.
Use automatic differentiation to obtain the first Piola–Kirchhoff stress and elasticity tensor directly from the scalar energy density.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements on a smooth benchmark, plus cut-independent coercivity and an O(h^-2) condition-number bound.
watch_item: Accuracy can still be limited by corner singularities at mixed Dirichlet–Neumann junctions unless local mesh refinement is used.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
